title: cni.lock file not relocated when XDG_DATA_DIR is set
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

If I set XDG_DATA_HOME=/foo, the backing store for the container is written to /foo; however, the cni.lock file is still written to $HOME/.config.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. XDG_DATA_HOME=/foo podman system migrate 

**Describe the results you received:**
$HOME/.config/cni/net.d/cni.lock

**Describe the results you expected:**
/foo/cni/net.d/cni.lock

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

No. Yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
I was unable to install btrfs-devel using dnf on this RHEL 8 system. Btrfs is necessary to compile the latest podman release. It's possible to relocate the cni.lock file to /foo/cni.lock by using the "--network-config-dir=/foo" flag, but I have to use that flag with every podman command or a copy of the file gets created in $HOME/.config.

comments.body: I believe you have to change XDG_CONFIG_DIR rather then XDG_DATA_DIR.  Reopen if this does not fix the problem.

With XDG_CONFIG_DIR=/foo, these files are still created in /tmp:
/tmp/.local
/tmp/.config
If you use XDG_DATA_HOME=/foo XDG_CONFIG_DIR=/foo, then only
/tmp/.config
gets created. I still have to use the "--network-config-dir=/foo" flag.
Also, is there a list of podman's XDG variables somewhere?
Please open a new issue on this.
